title: Normalize canonical Lenskit checkout after active sessions
goal: Attach /home/alex/repos/lenskit to the current main branch only after all active processes using the checkout are classified or finished, while preserving detached publication-source worktrees and any unique branch state.

acceptance.assertion: No Claude, Grabowski stdio, rLens or shell process has the canonical checkout as cwd or command target at the moment of branch attachment, unless explicitly classified safe and quiescent.
The canonical checkout is clean and HEAD equals origin/main; local main is fast-forwarded without reset, stash, rebase or loss of unique commits.
RepoBrief publication-source and tool worktrees remain detached and unchanged.
After attachment, branch is main, status is clean, HEAD equals origin/main, rLens/RepoBrief services remain healthy and active worktrees are unchanged.

metadata.live_finding: Canonical checkout was clean and exactly at origin/main but detached while multiple Claude, Grabowski stdio and rLens processes used it.
metadata.block_reason: The canonical checkout is clean and exactly at origin/main but eight live processes still use it as cwd, including two Claude/Python groups and shells. Attaching main now would mutate active working context.
metadata.next_action: Re-run typed checkout inventory after the active processes finish. Attach main only if process count is zero or every remaining shell is explicitly classified quiescent, HEAD still equals origin/main and the checkout remains clean.
